name: Threaded Code Dispatch
summary: Forth's classical dispatch styles: Direct Threaded Code (DTC, address array + NEXT routine), Indirect Threaded Code (ITC, one extra indirection enabling DOES> uniformly), Subroutine Threaded Code (STC, native CALL/RET dispatch). All share: a compiled word is a sequence of references to other words; what differs is what each reference resolves to.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The three classical Forth dispatch styles (DTC, ITC, STC) share a representation idea — a compiled word is a sequence of references to other words — and differ in *what each reference resolves to* and *who runs the dispatch*. Brad Rodriguez's "Moving Forth Part 1" is the canonical reference for all three.

sources.label: Brad Rodriguez — Moving Forth Part 1 (Threading models)

sections.title: Direct Threaded Code (DTC)
sections.content: **Direct Threaded Code** is the historical default and the simplest of the three: the threaded code is literally an array of machine-code addresses. The inner loop is fetch → jump; each primitive ends with `NEXT`. No per-instruction overhead beyond the address itself. Modern fallback in GForth/VFX when advanced techniques don't apply.

sections.title: Indirect Threaded Code (ITC)
sections.content: **Indirect Threaded Code** stores the address of a code-field word, which itself points to machine code — one extra indirection in the inner loop. ITC's extra indirection is what makes `DOES>` work uniformly: every word has a code field that the inner loop dispatches on, so primitives and user-defined words look identical to the dispatcher.

sections.title: Subroutine Threaded Code (STC)
sections.content: **Subroutine Threaded Code** stores native `CALL` instructions. The CPU's call/return machinery handles dispatch — no inner loop, no `NEXT`. Larger code size (a `CALL` is 5 bytes on x86-64 vs an 8-byte address slot, but pays once per invocation); on modern branch predictors, STC's call/return pattern outperforms ITC/DTC. Used heavily by SwiftForth, VFX Forth, Mecrisp, zeptoforth.
